questions.question: What are the four warning signs that VMC is occurring or about to occur?

options.text: Engine vibration, low oil pressure, high CHT, low fuel
Loss of directional control, stall warning horn, buffeting, rapid decay of control effectiveness
Airspeed decrease, altitude loss, heading change, bank angle
Rudder vibration, aileron flutter, nose drop, wing drop

questions.explanation: The four warning signs of VMC are: 1) Loss of directional control (full rudder and still turning), 2) Stall warning horn, 3) Buffeting before the stall, and 4) A rapid decay of control effectiveness.

questions.question: What two actions must occur simultaneously to recover from VMC?

options.text: Raise the gear and retract the flaps
Reduce power on the operating engine and pitch down
Apply full rudder and increase power
Feather the propeller and bank toward the good engine

questions.explanation: To recover from VMC, two simultaneous actions are required: 1) Reduce power on the operating engine (to reduce asymmetric thrust), and 2) Pitch down (to increase airspeed and make the rudder more effective).

questions.question: How does an increase in density altitude affect VMC speed?

options.text: VMC increases because the rudder becomes less effective
VMC decreases because less engine power means less asymmetrical thrust
VMC stays the same because it is an indicated airspeed
VMC increases because the airplane weighs less

questions.explanation: As density altitude increases, engine power decreases, resulting in less asymmetrical thrust. Less asymmetrical thrust means less yaw and less rudder needed, which decreases VMC. However, performance also decreases.

questions.question: At what bank angle toward the operating engine does the airplane achieve zero sideslip and minimum drag?

options.text: 0 degrees
2-3 degrees
5 degrees
8 degrees

questions.explanation: A 2-3 degree bank toward the operating engine results in a zero sideslip condition where the relative wind is parallel to the longitudinal axis. This produces the minimum drag and best performance with one engine inoperative.

questions.question: How does a forward C.G. affect VMC?

options.text: VMC increases because the airplane is heavier
VMC decreases because the longer arm to the rudder makes it more effective
VMC is not affected by C.G. position
VMC increases because more tail-down force is required

questions.explanation: A forward C.G. increases the arm (distance) between the C.G. and the rudder. A larger arm makes the rudder more effective, meaning less rudder is needed, leaving more available to the pilot and decreasing VMC.
